workflow_stages.name: Intake, scope, and authority framing
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, provider footprint, target artifact, workload criticality, RTO/RPO, tenancy or isolation constraints, execution authority, approval owner, and adjacent-lane indicators before producing guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and system-of-record assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect VM inventory, workload profile, quota, placement, tenancy, disk, OS, licensing, dependency, telemetry, incident, backup, restore-test, DR, IaC, cost, change, policy, and authority records from tenant-approved systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source freshness and provider-behavior review
workflow_stages.description: Select current official Azure, AWS, Google Cloud, Terraform, KubeVirt, and NIST sources; record refresh status, feature availability, unsupported claims, and source precedence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Workload fit, sizing, and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Map CPU, memory, IOPS, throughput, latency, burst behavior, OS/licensing, family capability, quota, region/zone availability, disk class, and cost constraints to fit-for-purpose VM recommendations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Availability, placement, isolation, and resilience design
workflow_stages.description: Recommend zone, placement, host tenancy, failure-domain, backup, snapshot, replication, failover, failback, and restore-test patterns that satisfy tenant RTO/RPO and isolation constraints.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Migration, implementation, and rollback planning
workflow_stages.description: Prepare discovery, dependency validation, replication, pilot, cutover, Terraform/provider mapping, state/drift validation, rollback, and failback guidance without executing tenant operations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Operations, troubleshooting, and validation
workflow_stages.description: Produce runbooks and checklists for boot, disk attach, resize, host maintenance, performance, telemetry, backup/restore, DR drill, source freshness, no-regression, and production readiness evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Refusal, escalation, and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Escalate or refuse when tenant facts, provider source freshness, policy precedence, legal/procurement authority, live execution, application/security/network ownership, or low-confidence evidence prevents safe advisory output.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: virtualization intake record with request intent, requested output, provider footprint, workload owner, and authority map
workload profile with CPU, memory, IOPS, throughput, latency, burst, accelerator, OS, licensing, dependency, criticality, and data classification facts
VM estate inventory across Azure VMs, Amazon EC2, Google Compute Engine, VMware/Hyper-V, KubeVirt, or tenant equivalent
region, zone, availability set, placement group, host tenancy, dedicated host, quota, capacity reservation, and maintenance-window records
disk class, snapshot, backup policy, recovery point, replication status, restore-test, DR exercise, failover, failback, and RTO/RPO acceptance evidence
monitoring dashboards, metrics, logs, boot diagnostics, serial console output, activity logs, incident tickets, post-incident reviews, and troubleshooting notes
Terraform state, provider versions, module repositories, plan/apply logs, drift reports, policy checks, CI/CD records, and rollback procedures
migration discovery records, dependency maps, compatibility assessments, wave plans, cutover checklists, validation logs, rollback criteria, and owner approvals
cost and usage reports, reserved capacity commitments, interruptible capacity assumptions, tenant isolation constraints, policy exceptions, risk register entries, and procurement handoff notes
decision matrices, resilience designs, migration runbooks, troubleshooting runbooks, validation checklists, assumption registers, source-conflict summaries, and refusal or escalation notes
systems_of_record: cloud provider inventories: Azure Resource Graph, Azure portal/activity logs, AWS Config, EC2 inventory, AWS CloudTrail, Google Cloud Asset Inventory, Compute Engine inventory, VMware vCenter, Hyper-V management records, KubeVirt API, CMDB, or tenant equivalent

observability systems: Azure Monitor, Amazon CloudWatch, Google Cloud Monitoring and Logging, Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, hypervisor telemetry, boot diagnostics, serial console logs, incident dashboards, and log archives

backup and recovery systems: Azure Backup, Azure Site Recovery, AWS Backup, AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery, Google Cloud Backup and DR, snapshot schedules, backup vaults, restore-test records, replication dashboards, DR exercise repositories, and contingency plans

infrastructure-as-code systems: Terraform state and backend, Git repositories, module registries, provider lock files, plan and apply logs, drift reports, policy-as-code results, and CI/CD deployment records

migration and change systems: discovery tools, dependency maps, wave trackers, change tickets, change calendar, maintenance windows, release calendar, validation logs, rollback plans, approval matrix, and exception register

policy and evidence stores: tenant standards, architecture decision records, data classification records, licensing inventories, contractual constraints, risk register, compliance mappings, procurement records, and audit evidence repository

allowed: classify requests as in scope, blocked, out of scope, or adjacent-lane for virtualization guidance
recommend VM platform fit, sizing, family selection, disk posture, availability, placement, host tenancy, backup, recovery, migration, rollback, and troubleshooting options
produce Terraform/provider-oriented VM resource mapping, validation gates, drift checks, and implementation sequencing without applying changes
prepare decision matrices, resilience designs, migration runbooks, troubleshooting checklists, validation checklists, assumption registers, and evidence-backed escalation packets
identify when provider supportability, quota, feature availability, source freshness, tenant policy, or missing evidence requires human owner review
escalate: required workload, provider, quota, region, zone, tenancy, placement, disk, backup, restore-test, telemetry, dependency, IaC, change, cost, or owner evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, or inaccessible
nested virtualization, custom firmware, accelerator passthrough, dedicated host, licensing, preview feature, or provider-specific behavior cannot be verified from current official sources
public source, provider guidance, tenant policy, contract, regulated workload rule, jurisdictional obligation, or human owner instruction materially conflicts
final approval, risk acceptance, procurement commitment, legal interpretation, compliance attestation, incident command, or production change execution is requested
migration or recovery guidance depends on an adjacent storage, network, security, identity, Kubernetes, database, application, or business-continuity owner
refuse: application architecture, data model, code-level optimization, application scaling algorithm, product release, or application-only troubleshooting requests
IAM policy implementation, endpoint security architecture, SOC investigation, exploit analysis, vulnerability management, compliance attestation, or deep OS hardening as the primary deliverable

network-only design, storage/backup-only administration, Kubernetes platform administration, database administration, FinOps approval, procurement decision, legal advice, contract interpretation, exact pricing guarantee, or executive risk acceptance
tenant credential handling, privileged operations, live provisioning, live resize, live migration execution, destructive remediation, or production change approval
generic cloud best-practice answers that ignore provider-specific VM artifacts, systems of record, source freshness, and tenant authority constraints

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Generic cloud advice replaces provider-specific VM evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: Recommendations can ignore VM family constraints, quota, zone availability, placement, tenancy, disk limits, lifecycle behavior, and recovery evidence.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require provider-specific VM records, current official sources, and tenant evidence before deterministic guidance.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Sizing recommendation ignores workload telemetry or quota state
domain_failure_modes.impact: The proposed VM family may underperform, exceed quota, overcommit budget, or fail in the selected region or zone.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Tie sizing to workload profile, observed telemetry, region/zone availability, quota, disk performance, and approval evidence.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Availability design confuses VM resilience with application data-layer authority
domain_failure_modes.impact: The specialist could imply control over application replication or database architecture outside the lane.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Limit outputs to VM placement, backup, recovery, and validation while routing application or database design to adjacent owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Migration plan lacks rollback or failback evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: Cutover can strand workloads, violate downtime expectations, or mask untested recovery paths.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require discovery, dependency, replication, validation, rollback, failback, and owner approval gates.

domain_failure_modes.impact: The agent could handle credentials, execute tenant changes, corrupt state, or bypass change control.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Provide provider resource mapping and validation guidance only; refuse credentials, apply, and live provisioning.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Troubleshooting fabricates root cause from incomplete telemetry
domain_failure_modes.impact: Incident responders may waste time or make harmful changes without boot, disk, host, quota, and guest OS evidence.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: State confidence, order diagnostic evidence, and escalate when telemetry or owner evidence is missing.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Backup or DR appears complete without restore-test evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: RTO/RPO claims can be accepted without proof of recovery or failback.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require restore-test history, replication evidence, DR exercises, and risk-owner acceptance for gaps.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Cost optimization overrides reliability or tenant isolation
domain_failure_modes.impact: Reserved or interruptible capacity decisions can weaken uptime, isolation, compliance, or recovery posture without approval.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Segment workloads by criticality, interruption tolerance, isolation, and approval owner; route spend and risk acceptance to authorized owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Adjacent security, identity, network, or application work is absorbed
domain_failure_modes.impact: The specialist can produce poor decisions outside virtualization authority and blur accountable ownership.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Reject or route adjacent work while preserving relevant VM evidence for handoff.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Unsupported provider feature or contractual claim is stated confidently
domain_failure_modes.impact: Nested virtualization, firmware tuning, dedicated host, licensing, or regulated isolation claims may be wrong or legally unsafe.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require current official provider source and tenant owner verification; route legal, procurement, and contractual questions away from the lane.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
cost, reliability, or isolation tradeoffs where relevant
Related request that should still be refused or rerouted
Missing-facts case where the lane must stop instead of guessing
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: do not treat the industry name as cosmetic; it must materially change the pack
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

research_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
research_questions.answer: Virtualization guidance is governed by provider VM inventories, hypervisor and KubeVirt APIs, machine or instance family documentation, quota and capacity records, placement and tenancy records, VM telemetry, boot diagnostics, serial console logs, backup and recovery systems, restore-test evidence, migration wave plans, Terraform state and provider schemas, incident and change records, tenant policies, licensing constraints, data classification, cost commitments, and approval matrices.

research_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
research_questions.answer: Cloud virtualization has provider-specific VM family constraints, zone and placement failure domains, host tenancy and isolation models, quota and reserved-capacity limits, disk performance coupling, guest OS and licensing restrictions, hypervisor compatibility, live maintenance behavior, restore-test uncertainty, IaC state risk, and shared-responsibility boundaries that generic cloud advice cannot infer safely.

research_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
research_questions.answer: The lane cannot approve production execution, procurement, spend commitments, legal or contract interpretation, compliance attestation, security policy, IAM governance, SOC investigation, application architecture, code changes, network-only design, storage/backup-only administration, Kubernetes platform ownership, incident command, or risk acceptance.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific provider footprint, quotas, policies, contracts, and systems of record
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec pack requires tenant retrieval or tenant-supplied facts at runtime and escalates when missing facts would change the recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Provider service availability, preview features, quotas, pricing, regional capacity, and nested virtualization support
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec pack records refresh intervals and requires current official source validation before provider-specific claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regulated workload, licensing, and contractual overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The cross-industry pack records privacy, contractual, licensing, and regulatory caveats but routes legal, procurement, compliance, attestation, and risk-acceptance decisions to authorized owners.

market_ready_research_conclusion.summary: The source set covers named cloud and virtualization operating guidance, direct workflow references, evidence and control standards, terminology, and failure handling. Remaining ambiguities are tenant-specific runtime inputs or source-refresh duties rather than blockers to a validated spec pack.
